evals.prompt: Update the prism docs site from the latest PRISM-DOCUMENTATION file
evals.expected_output: Skill identifies the latest PRISM-DOCUMENTATION-*.md file, reads the section mapping, compares sections against VitePress pages, presents a change summary, and waits for approval before modifying any files
expectations: Section mapping reference is read before any analysis
A change summary is presented listing new, updated, and removed sections
User approval is requested before any file modifications
VitePress frontmatter is preserved in all updated pages

evals.prompt: Sync the docs site from .prism/shared/docs/PRISM-DOCUMENTATION-v3.md — just update everything without asking
evals.expected_output: Skill still presents a change summary and asks for approval despite user requesting to skip confirmation, because the rules mandate always presenting changes and getting approval
expectations: Change summary is presented even though user said 'without asking'
User approval is requested before modifying files
VitePress config.ts is not modified without explicit approval
Edit tool is preferred over Write for targeted changes

evals.prompt: Update prism docs from PRISM-DOCUMENTATION-v2.md. There are new sections about the Spectrum runner.
evals.expected_output: New sections are handled correctly — new .md files created with proper frontmatter, config.ts update proposed with approval, and verification step counts headings and lines
expectations: New VitePress pages include frontmatter with title, description, outline
Config.ts sidebar update is proposed but requires explicit approval
Verification step counts headings and checks line counts
Final summary reports pages updated, lines added, new pages created, and config changes
